steps.narration: The page shows a green heading "Quarterly Revenue Report" that needs to be typed into the answer box.
The screen shows a task panel with four buttons: Cancel Order, Confirm Order, Save Draft, and Delete Item.
The page shows a shipping method dropdown currently set to "-- Choose shipping method --" with a Submit button below.
A contact form is displayed with empty fields for Full Name and Email Address, along with a Submit button.
The name field is filled with "Jane Peterson" and the email field is still empty, ready to be filled.
The page shows a "Task: Agree and Submit" form with an unchecked checkbox labeled "I agree to the terms and conditions" and a Submit button.
The checkbox is now checked (shown with a blue checkmark), and the Submit button is ready to be clicked.
The table shows Wireless Mouse priced at $34.50.
The navigation menu shows five links: Dashboard, My Profile, Account Settings, Billing History, and Help & Support.
The shopping list shows 7 items: Whole wheat bread, Organic milk, Free-range eggs, Bananas, Chicken breast, Brown rice, and Olive oil.
The page shows a "Task: Copy Text" form with source field containing "TXN-2024-08-ALPHA" and an empty destination field.
The page shows a paragraph about the Greenfield Community Library, founded by Margaret Thornton, with an answer input box and Submit button.
The customer contact card shows Robert Tanaka's phone number as (415) 555-0182.
The input box currently shows "(415" which is incomplete - need to fill the complete phone number.
The input box still shows "(415" which is incomplete, so I need to clear it and fill the complete phone number.
The input field shows "(415" selected/highlighted after the triple_click, ready to be replaced with the full phone number.
A support ticket form is shown with the issue about a double-charged invoice, and I need to select the "Billing" category.
The "Billing" radio button is now selected (shown with a filled blue circle), and the Submit button is ready to be clicked.
The Display Settings table shows Refresh rate is 144 Hz.
The order summary shows two items: USB-C Cable at $12.50 and Laptop Stand at $37.25, with a text input box for the total.
A pizza order form with five unchecked checkboxes (Mushroom, Pepperoni, Olive, Pepper, Anchovy) and a Place Order button is displayed.
Mushroom checkbox is now checked (shown in blue), while Olive and Pepper still need to be selected.
Mushroom and Olive are now checked; Pepper still needs to be selected.
A 3x3 grid of numbered buttons (1-9) is displayed, with the task asking to click the button labelled '5'.
The user profile shows the email address "ariel.nguyen@northpine.io" which needs to be typed into the answer box.
The page shows a quantity selector currently set to 1 with increment/decrement buttons and an "Add to Cart" button.
The quantity input is selected showing value "1" and needs to be changed to 4.
The quantity input now shows 4, and the Add to Cart button is ready to be clicked.
The pricing table shows four plans: Basic ($8.99), Standard ($14.99), Pro ($24.99), and Lite ($4.99), with Lite being the cheapest.
The list shows Brassroot Co. was founded in 2015.
The page shows a dropdown to choose a color with the current value "—" and a Confirm button.
The project notice shows the hardware procurement window for Q3 closed on June 14th, which is the procurement deadline date.
The shipping rate table shows Standard shipping in Zone B is $11.
A shipping address form is displayed with empty fields for Street, City, State, and ZIP, along with a "Save Address" button.
The street field now shows "240 Maple Ave" filled in, and the city, state, and ZIP fields remain empty.
The city field now shows "Portland" filled in, with the state dropdown and ZIP field still empty.
The state dropdown now shows "OR" selected, with the ZIP field still empty and ready to be filled.
All four fields are now filled in correctly with the complete address, and the Save Address button is ready to be clicked.
A form asking to calculate sales tax with a subtotal of $80.00 at 7.5% tax rate is displayed.
The table shows engineers Sara Patel (4 years), Ana Roth (11 years), and Tom Vega (2 years); Ana Roth has the longest tenure among engineers.
A password form is shown requiring at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one digit, and one symbol from !@#$.
The password field shows "Password1!" which meets all requirements (8+ chars, uppercase, digit, symbol), and the Set Password button is ready to click.
The screen shows a 2FA setup form with four radio button options: SMS, Email, Authenticator app, and Hardware key, plus an "Enable 2FA" button.
The Authenticator app radio button is now selected (shown with filled blue circle), and the Enable 2FA button is ready to be clicked.
The "Enable 2FA" button has been clicked and a "Confirm activation?" prompt with a "Confirm" button has appeared below.
The invoice shows three rows: Widget A (3 × $8.00 = $24.00), Widget B (2 × $11.50 = $23.00), Service Fee (1 × $5.25 = $5.25), totaling $52.25.
The Privacy Settings page shows Three checkboxes: Two-Step Login (OFF→ON, unchecked), Activity Sharing (ON→OFF, checked), and Marketing Emails (OFF→ON, unchecked).
Two-Step Login is now checked, Activity Sharing is checked (needs to be unchecked), and Marketing Emails is unchecked (needs to be checked).
Two-Step Login is checked, Activity Sharing is unchecked, and Marketing Emails still needs to be checked before saving.
All three checkboxes are now in the correct state: Two-Step Login checked, Activity Sharing unchecked, and Marketing Emails checked, ready to save.
A meeting scheduling form is displayed with empty fields for date, time, and attendee email.
The date field is filled with "2026-06-15" and the time and attendee email fields remain empty.
The time field now shows "14:30" filled in, and the attendee email field remains empty.
All three fields are filled correctly with date "2026-06-15", time "14:30", and attendee email "mariah@firm.io", and the Schedule button is ready to be clicked.
The refund policy states 25% restocking fee applies between days 15-30; day 22 falls in this range, so 25% of $200.00 = $50.00 = 5000 cents.
The email transcript shows a reference number "RX-7B4K-29CT" in the message body.

The loan eligibility form shows rules requiring age ≥21, income ≥$45,000, and credit score ≥680; the applicant is 27 years old with $52,000 income but credit score 663, which fails the credit score re
A form is displayed asking to convert 7.5 kilometers to meters, with a text input field and Submit button.
The leaderboard shows player_yellow has the highest score of 2050.
The page shows five stocks with their closing prices: AAPL ($182.50), GE ($74.10), MSFT ($422.30), T ($18.95), and AMD ($124.40), all with unchecked checkboxes.
AAPL is now checked (price $182.50 > $100); next I need to check MSFT ($422.30) and AMD ($124.40).
AAPL and MSFT are now checked; AMD ($124.40) still needs to be checked.
The page shows a query "how do I revoke API access for an old service account?" with four radio button options: billing, api-credentials, onboarding, and logs.
The "api-credentials" radio button is now selected (shown with blue fill), and the Submit button is ready to be clicked.
The page shows a form asking to compute a final invoice amount from a $480.00 subtotal with 10% discount, 6% tax, and $12 shipping fee.
The page shows three routes with R-Bravo being closed on weekends (today is Saturday) and R-Charlie being hazmat-only (cargo is non-hazardous), making R-Alpha the only legal route at 340km.
The page shows a ROT-13 encoded message "URYYB JBEYQ" that needs to be decoded and typed in uppercase.
The page shows 5 items with prices: A=$35, B=$20, C=$15, D=$10, E=$5, and I need to find a combination summing to $50 (B+C+D+E = $20+$15+$10+$5 = $50, or A+D+E = $35+$10+$5 = $50).
Item A ($35) is now checked, and I need to also select D ($10) and E ($5) to reach exactly $50.
Items A ($35) and D ($10) are checked, totaling $45, so I need to also check E ($5) to reach exactly $50.
The email reply form is visible with fields for To, Subject, and body, along with the original email showing sender is alex.romero@example.com.

steps.action: fill(#subj, "Re: missing license key for invoice INV-9821")
steps.narration: The To field is now filled with "alex.romero@example.com" and the Subject and body fields remain empty.

steps.action: fill(#body, "Dear Alex, thank you for reaching out. I'm sorry to hear you did not receive your activation key for invoice INV-9821. I will resend the license key to you shortly. Please let me know if
steps.narration: The To and Subject fields are filled correctly; the body textarea is empty and needs to be filled with text mentioning INV-9821.
All three fields are filled correctly - To, Subject starting with 'Re:', and body mentioning INV-9821, ready to send.
A number sequence puzzle shows "2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?" with a text input field and Submit button.
The triage table shows two critical tickets: T-002 (2026-04-22) and T-004 (2026-02-11); T-004 is older so it should be addressed first.
The page shows a currency conversion task with rates: 1 USD = 0.92 EUR, 1 EUR = 142 JPY, asking to convert $250 USD to JPY.
The page shows a field mapping task where 'dob' from the source record needs to map to a target field, with 'birthdate' being the obvious match in the target record.
The page shows a median computation task with numbers 4, 11, 9, 22, 7, 18, 6 and an empty text input field.
The graph problem shows edges with weights, and I need to find the shortest path from A to D.
The audit log shows delete actions performed by alice (09:18) and bob (09:24), making 2 distinct users who performed delete actions.
